name: Nannou
description: Creative-coding framework for generative art and interactive visuals, usable for games

kind.comment: Creative-coding toolkit inspired by Processing / openFrameworks / Cinder; games and interactive visuals are a secondary use of its generative-art focus.

license.comment: Dual-licensed MIT OR Apache-2.0 per crates.io metadata (the GitHub API license field is unset, but crate metadata and repo LICENSE files confirm the dual license).

documentation.comment: Official nannou Guide (mdBook) with getting-started and tutorials; API docs also on docs.rs.

latest-stable-version.comment: Newest stable on crates.io as of 2026-07-06, released 2026-06-22; 0.20 is a major rewrite that re-bases nannou on the Bevy engine.

maintenance-status.comment: 0.20.0 released 2026-06-22 (last push 2026-06-23). Cadence is bursty: there was a ~2.5-year gap between 0.19.0 (Jan 2024) and 0.20.0 (Jun 2026), the latter a large Bevy-based rewrite.

maturity.comment: Established (~9 years) and used in real art installations/live visuals, but still pre-1.0 (0.20.x) and the 0.20 rewrite onto Bevy is a significant, still-settling API break.

backing.value: Community — led by the original authors (Mitchell Nordine et al.) under the nannou-org org; funded via Open Collective
backing.comment: No company/foundation; community project with Open Collective sponsorship.

implementation.comment: Pure Rust: built on Bevy and wgpu (both pure Rust) with only platform glue; no engine *-sys bindings.

architecture.comment: Nannou's user-facing model is immediate-mode (a Model state redrawn each frame via the Draw API, Processing-style). Since 0.20 its crates are exposed as Bevy plugins, so Bevy's ECS is the underlying foundation; immediate-mode is the dominant nannou-facing surface.

built-in-ecs.comment: The primary nannou model is the sketch/app Draw loop, not ECS. Bevy's ECS is available underneath (nannou 0.20 apps are Bevy apps) but is not how a nannou user typically structures work.

dimensionality.comment: 2D vector drawing is the primary focus; the Draw API also supports basic 3D (meshes, camera), and full 3D is reachable through the underlying Bevy engine.

scripting.comment: Sketches/apps are written in Rust; no embedded scripting language.

data-driven-scenes.comment: No nannou scene/prefab file format; visuals are built in code. Bevy's scene serialization is technically accessible underneath but not part of nannou's toolkit.

reflection-serialization.comment: Nannou exposes no first-class reflection/scene serialization of its own; serde is used for some config. Bevy Reflect exists in the underlying engine but is not surfaced as a nannou feature.

hot-reload.comment: No documented nannou code/asset hot-reload story; the underlying Bevy AssetServer supports asset hot-reload if used directly, but nannou does not advertise it.

visual-editor.comment: Code-first creative-coding toolkit; ships no graphical scene/level editor.

live-inspector.comment: No first-party runtime world/entity inspector. Because 0.20 apps are Bevy apps, community tools like bevy-inspector-egui could in principle be added, but this is not a nannou feature.

asset-pipeline.comment: Async loading of common formats (images via the image crate, audio, fonts) from an assets folder; on 0.20 this sits atop Bevy's AssetServer. No import/reprocessing pipeline surfaced by nannou itself.

cli-project-tooling.comment: No official scaffolding CLI; the guide starts projects with plain cargo. Community starter templates exist (e.g. nannou-web-template).

debug-profiling-tools.comment: No first-party profiler/diagnostics overlay. Bevy's diagnostics/Tracy integration is reachable via the underlying engine but is not exposed as a nannou feature.

ide-support.comment: Nothing beyond standard Rust tooling (rust-analyzer just works).

rendering-backend.comment: Renders through wgpu (nannou_wgpu, and Bevy's wgpu-based renderer since 0.20), which abstracts Vulkan/Metal/DX12/GL/WebGPU.

pbr.comment: Nannou's Draw API is unlit; it has no PBR pipeline of its own. PBR is only reachable by dropping down to the underlying Bevy engine directly.

lighting.comment: The Draw API renders unlit 2D/3D; nannou provides no first-party lighting system. Bevy's lighting is accessible if you use Bevy directly.

shadows.comment: No first-party shadow support in nannou's Draw API.

custom-shaders.comment: Custom shaders supported: nannou_wgpu gives direct wgpu access for authoring WGSL shaders and render pipelines.

sprites-tilemaps.comment: Not a sprite/tilemap-oriented toolkit; images can be drawn via draw.texture, but there is no sprite-batching or tilemap system.

particle-system.comment: No built-in particle/VFX system; particle effects are hand-rolled with the Draw API.

post-processing.comment: No first-party post-processing stack; effects must be hand-rolled with render targets and custom shaders (or Bevy's pipeline directly).

text-rendering.comment: Built-in text drawing via the Draw API (draw.text) with font loading and glyph layout.

physics.comment: No physics in nannou; bring-your-own (e.g. rapier), or use a Bevy physics plugin since 0.20 apps are Bevy apps.

audio.comment: Ships nannou_audio (CPAL-based) for audio hosts, devices, and input/output streams.

input.comment: First-party keyboard and mouse events in the app model. Touch and gamepad are reachable through the underlying winit/Bevy input but are not highlighted as nannou features.

animation.comment: Provides easing/tween helpers (depends on pennereq, i.e. Penner easing equations); no skeletal or sprite-frame animation system.

ui-system.comment: UI via egui (0.20 depends on bevy_egui; earlier releases shipped nannou_egui). Historically nannou used conrod.

networking.comment: No game networking/replication. nannou_osc and MIDI crates provide OSC/MIDI messaging for art installations, not multiplayer.

gltf-model-import.comment: Nannou's Draw API does not import glTF/GLB; standard model loading is only available by using the underlying Bevy engine directly.

macos.comment: Native macOS target supported (Metal via wgpu).

web-wasm.comment: No official/documented WASM support; community templates (e.g. nannou-web-template) exist, and the underlying Bevy engine can target WASM, but nannou does not officially support or test web builds as of 2026-07-06.

clean-build-time.comment: Not independently measured this pass. Since 0.20 nannou depends on the full Bevy engine (bevy 0.19), so a clean release build is expected to be heavy (Bevy-class), well above classic lightweight nannou; no verified number on a stated machine.

incremental-build-time.comment: Not measured. With the Bevy foundation the dependency graph is large; prefer null over a guess.

binary-size.comment: Not measured. Bevy-based apps trend moderate-to-large, but no stripped-binary measurement was taken this pass.

web-bundle-size.comment: Web is not an officially supported target, so no representative wasm-opt bundle size to report.

runtime-overhead.comment: Not benchmarked. On the Bevy foundation there is an ECS scheduler/app loop underneath, so idle overhead is higher than classic lightweight frameworks, but no measurement was taken; prefer null over a guess.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time crates.io downloads of the nannou crate as of 2026-07-06.

last-release.comment: Release date of the newest stable version 0.20.0.

shipped-titles.comment: No notable released games widely documented; nannou is used for generative art, live visuals, and interactive installations rather than shipped game titles.

community-size.comment: Active creative-coding community with a dedicated chat (Slack/Matrix), but a small core development team and bursty contribution cadence. ~6.7k GitHub stars as of 2026-07-06.

plugin-asset-ecosystem.comment: Companion crates are mostly first-party (nannou_audio, nannou_laser, nannou_osc, nannou_video, nannou_wgpu); little third-party plugin ecosystem. Since 0.20 the broader Bevy plugin ecosystem is technically reachable.

learning-resources.comment: Official nannou Guide (mdBook) plus an extensive examples directory and creative-coding tutorials; fewer game-oriented videos, and some material may lag the 0.20 Bevy rewrite.
